expectations.text: Case focus is explicitly covered: REPORT_STATE and resume semantics remain stable
expectations.evidence: In result.md, the run explicitly covers the case focus: it states the focus as “REPORT_STATE and resume semantics remain stable” and provides explicit enumerations of REPORT_STATE values (FINAL_EXISTS, DRAFT_EXISTS, CONTEXT_ONLY, FRESH) and ties them to user options, plus stable mode semantics: “full_regenerate… Next: Phase 0”, “smart_refresh… Next: Phase 2”, and “reuse / resume… Continue from current state. No reset.” It also notes apply_user_choice.sh as the deterministic mechanism.

expectations.text: Output aligns with primary phase phase0
expectations.evidence: result.md is organized around “What was verified (phase0 contract alignment)” and “Alignment with primary phase: phase0,” listing Phase0 responsibilities (initialize runtime state, classify REPORT_STATE, normalize inputs, lock policy, produce phase0 artifacts, and prompt/apply routing when REPORT_STATE indicates existing artifacts). It explicitly avoids later-phase claims and states verification is limited to phase0 contract due to missing runtime artifacts.
